text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I arrive at summer camp determined to help
Manhattan Cafe recover from all her recent
paranormal encounters.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The students, on the other hand, arrive ready to
have some fun.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
Hehe... Summer camp is going to be so much fun.
Where should we go?

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
The abandoned cliff? Oh, but the forgotten cave
hidden in the mountains could be good too.
Hehe...

text_block_list.name: Student A
text_block_list.text:
W-wait, who are you talking to, Cafe?! You're
giving me the creeps!

text_block_list.name: Student B
text_block_list.text:
Haha... I don't really get your deal, but I feel like
I'm in a horror movie whenever you're around...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
I'd say it's more mystery than horror, myself.
Don't you agree, Trainer?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
It's far trickier to investigate an unseen entity
than a material monster, so let's hope nobody
interferes. Besides...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
I'm more interested in what it's trying to do than
what it actually is.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
We still know next to nothing about Manhattan
Cafe's friend.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I'm usually too busy to do much digging, but
summer camp could be my chance...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...Are you all right? You're staring at me.
choice_data_list: This summer, I'd like to... talk about things.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Getting to know her better could open up more
paths to improvement. At least, I think it could.
